arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola optimistic Man City will finish strongly in top-five battle
arts.sum: City are in a fight to qualify for the Champions League.

arts.ttl: MARTIN KEOWN TALKS TACTICS: Pep Guardiola must give back old Man City canvas to his main artist Kevin De Bruyne to break down Crystal Palace
arts.sum: MARTIN KEOWN: Guardiola has won titles in different ways but has predominantly stuck to a system with wingers to pin back opposition defences and create a wide canvas for De Bruyne.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola admits Man City have 'seven finals' left in the Premier League as the Spaniard seeks to avoid missing Champions League for the first time in 17 years
arts.sum: Pep Guardiola admitted Manchester City now have seven cup finals to qualify for the Champions League after losing their advantage in the race for Europe.

arts.ttl: Man City dealt major fresh injury doubt for Crystal Palace
arts.sum: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola could be without six players for the Premier League game against Crystal Palace

arts.ttl: Glasner highlights Kamada impact v Brighton and elements of squad rotation
arts.sum: Speaking in his pre-Manchester City press conference, manager Oliver Glasner was full of praise for Daichi Kamada and the team as a whole for their recent performances and adaptability amidst injuries, suspension and fixture congestion.

arts.ttl: Guardiola: Manchester City need ‘near perfection’ to make Champions League
arts.sum: City have seven games left before visit of Crystal Palace ‘It’s not complicated, you have to win almost all games’ Pep Guardiola has told his players they need to be almost perfect in their final seven Premier League games if they are to qualify for the Champions League. City have the opportunity to put pressure on their rivals for a top-five place by playing first this weekend. Crystal Palace visit the Etihad Stadium on Saturday lunchtime and a City would jump two places to fourth with a win, temporarily at least. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Crystal Palace striker looks set to wear ear protection for the rest of the season
arts.sum: Crystal Palace boss Oliver Glasner is expecting Jean-Philippe Mateta to have to play with his ear protection for the rest of the season. Mateta has been playing with his mask since needing 25 stitches to a severe laceration to his ear following a collision outside of the box with Millwall keeper Liam Roberts in the fifth round of the FA Cup.

arts.ttl: Crystal Palace owner sued for £6million by former Wolves manager in remarkable lawsuit
arts.sum: Bruno Lage, who managed Wolves until October 2022, has sued Crystal Palace co-owner John Textor after thinking that he'd be offered the job at Selhurst Park or French giants Lyon

arts.ttl: Paul Merson says something 'unbelievable' has now happened which is 'certainly bad news' for Arsenal and Man City
arts.sum: Liverpool, Arsenal and Manchester City have all taken it in turns to dominate the upper echelons of the Premier League over the past seven or...

arts.ttl: EXCLUSIVE: Man City open Anatoliy Trubin talks as goalkeeper shake-up looms
arts.sum: FootballTransfers understands that Manchester City have opened talks with Benfica ’s rising star Anatoliy Trubin as Pep Guardiola’s side prepare for a possible double departure in the goalkeeper department this summer. EXCLUSIVE: Man City open Anatoliy Trubin talks as goalkeeper shake-up looms
